title: Christella × jerdonii
taxonomy: Christella × jerdonii is classified as a hybrid species within the family Thelypteridaceae. It is a hybrid between the species Christella dentata and Christella japonica. The classification of Christella × jerdonii reflects its unique genetic makeup, which combines characteristics from its parent species. As a hybrid, Christella × jerdonii is not assigned a distinct genus but is instead classified within the genus Christella. The hybrid status of Christella × jerdonii highlights the complex relationships within the Christella genus and its family.

intro: Christella × jerdonii, also known as the hybrid lady fern, is a rare and endangered species of fern that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.
